message: 🌱 Add caddy: Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive mission (#470)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "caddy-7192-allow-global-dns-resolvers-directive",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "caddy: Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive",
+    "description": "Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive. This issue affects 10+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify caddy feature symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your caddy deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=caddy\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors related to: Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check current resource configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant caddy resources:\n```bash\nkubectl get resource -A\nkubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>\n```\nInside of a `tls` block you can put the `resolvers` directive to specify the resolvers used for DNS ACME challenge. With the new global `dns` setting, the `resolvers` setting should also have a global"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive",
+        "description": "- Added `tls_resolvers` global option that accepts one or more DNS resolver addresses\n- Global resolvers are stored in the TLS app configuration\n- Resolvers are automatically applied to ACME issuers that use DNS challenges\n- Local `resolvers` directive in `tls` blocks can override global settings\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The root cause is: - Added `tls_resolvers` global option that accepts one or more DNS resolver addresses\n- Global resolvers are stored in the TLS app configuration\n- Resolvers are automatically applied to ACME issuers that use DNS challenges\n- Local `resolvers` directive in `tls` blocks can override global settings. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
